question: Create a `ResourceHandler` class that manages a dynamically allocated integer array. Its constructor should allocate an array of a specified size. Implement a destructor that correctly deallocates the dynamically allocated memory to prevent memory leaks.

test_cases.expected:
Creating handler1 with size 3
handler1 array: [10, 20, 30]
Creating handler2 as a copy of handler1 (deep copy)
handler2 array: [10, 20, 30]
Modifying handler1...
handler1 array after modification: [99, 20, 30]
handler2 array (should be unchanged due to deep copy): [10, 20, 30]
Creating handler3 and assigning handler1 to it (deep copy)
handler3 initial array: [5, 10]
handler3 array after assignment from handler1: [99, 20, 30]
End of test case. Destructors will be called correctly.
